rawIntent: Phase 1: Core domain models and repository interfaces. Generate ONLY TypeScript interfaces and types — no classes, no implementations, no database imports, no SQL queries. All model interfaces must use exact attribute names and types from the canonical definitions: Employee (id, employeeNumber, firstName, lastName, email, managerId, department, hireDate, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt), LeavePolicy (id, policyName, leaveType: 'annual'|'sick'|'emergency'|'unpaid'|'maternity'|'paternity', entitlementDays, maxConsecutiveDays, minNoticeDays, requiresApproval, carryOverLimit, validityStart, validityEnd, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt), LeaveBalance (id, employeeId, policyId, balanceYear, availableDays, usedDays, pendingDays, carriedOverDays, createdAt, updatedAt), LeaveRequest (id, employeeId, policyId, startDate, endDate, totalDays, status: LeaveRequestStatus, reason, managerId, managerNotes, reviewedAt, createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt), CreateLeaveRequestDto (employeeId, policyId, startDate, endDate, reason), UpdateLeaveRequestDto (status, managerNotes), Notification (id, recipientId, senderId, type: NotificationType, title, body, metadata, isRead, readAt, createdAt). Repository interfaces must define ONLY method signatures: EmployeeRepository (findById, findByEmail, save, update, softDelete), PolicyRepository (findById, findActiveByLeaveType, save, update, softDelete), BalanceRepository (findByEmployeeAndYear, findByEmployeePolicyAndYear, save, update), LeaveRepository (findById, findByEmployee, findByManager, save, update, softDelete), NotificationRepository (findByRecipient, save, markAsRead). Do NOT emit any concrete classes, constructors, pg/Pool imports, query strings, or implementation logic. Include unit tests at tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.model.test.ts and tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.test.ts.

successCriteria.description: All domain model interfaces (Employee, LeavePolicy, LeaveBalance, LeaveRequest, CreateLeaveRequestDto, UpdateLeaveRequestDto, Notification) are created with exact field names and types from canonical definitions
All repository interfaces (EmployeeRepository, PolicyRepository, BalanceRepository, LeaveRepository, NotificationRepository) are created with exact method signatures specified
No concrete classes, constructors, database imports (pg/Pool), SQL queries, or implementation logic are generated
Unit test files are created at tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.model.test.ts and tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.test.ts

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces
Input validation: Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled
Modular monolith architecture with strict dependency direction
LeaveRequest lifecycle states are Pending, Approved, and Rejected
Balance deductions occur only during approveLeaveRequest
Notifications are created on submission, approval, and rejection events
outOfScope: Any files outside the specified domain interfaces, repository interfaces, and the two test files
